response.justification: Strong technical and open-source profile with ~15 years of broad software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, plus genuine Linux/Unix depth and a founder role on an open-source geo-data platform that aligns well with the company's digital-sovereignty focus. However, the role requires German at C1 minimum and the candidate lists only basic German, which is a serious gap for a client-facing executive position in the German market. Formal team leadership and technical project management are also under-evidenced (current and recent titles are individual-contributor engineering roles), and key desired skills (Python, UML) are absent. Worth a conversation given the open-source alignment, but the language and leadership gaps are material.
strengths: Deep open-source and Linux/Unix experience matching the digital-sovereignty focus
Broad ~15-year engineering background plus strong mentoring/teaching track record
Founder of an open-source platform showing architecture and ownership
concerns: German only 'Basic' vs. required C1 minimum
Limited evidence of formal team leadership / technical project management
Missing key desired skills: Python and UML/modeling not shown
